The pictures can’t capture the amazing energy and love from all the participants and their enjoyment going though the #CodeJam !
Many thanks to the volunteer photographer (I included some of her pics, abrigado!)

The organizers of the SAP Community Curitiba deserve all the credit, from @SimoneAzevedo   to @LuizSouza_cwb   and @AdrianoCWB .  Such a well organized event. I can’t wait to be back in Curitiba but now I’m off to #Campinas for an SAP Inside Track. SAP Community  is strong!

____

Stop number two of the LATAM roadshow for me was an amazing trip to a Campinas, a city just north to the north of São Paulo. The event is an SAP inside track (SAP Inside Track Campinas Inside Track Brasil 🇧🇷 just outside of Sao Paulo ) and I get to connect with my colleagues @antonia_zorn @noravonthenen and @Vitaliy-R  , as well as continue with @RichHeilman and @thomas_jung , but unfortunately for Rich and Thimas, this will be their last stop of the tour.

 

Also exciting is getting to meet so many community members, especially our SAP champions @RobertaFernande  , @MRobalinho , and @Marssel700 . Truly, there were so many volunteers and an amazing campus that hosted our event lots of great sponsors and familiar faces form the last two years. Kicking off today with a co-presentation with Antonia was fun and then watching my colleagues present, as well as so many other customers and partners from the SAP community. It was so many people learning and sharing about SAP  in the Brazil community, it’s just something that I cannot express enough, how vibrant the Volunteer SAP Community  is and the interest of the attendees. The food was amazing. The capybara‘s were amazing and I appreciated every conversation and photo, but I’m sure you could tell that….


Next, we got together for a meet up at an amazing local brewery. I don’t even think there was a way to count the number of people who came over and enjoyed time together socializing.


But like all good things this stop had to come to an end so me, Nora, Vitaliy and Antonia packed up and are off to our next stop Porto Alegre. See you there!
_____

My 3rd stop of the LATAM CodeJam tour was continuing with Vitaliy Rudnytskiy  , Nora von Thenen  and Antonia Zorn  to São Leopoldo/ Porto Alegre. This southern Brazil city and its people are amazing but wow the SAP Labs office is sooooo amazing.
 

 


We had two CodeJams and a meetup between Monday/Tuesday. A packed house both days. And a special shoutout to our local support who’ve been with me in Curitiba and will also join at my last sip of Recife. Vitor Ugo Fiori  Vitória Cristofolli.

 

 

 

 
The CodeJams were amazing and the attendees so great at participating and engaging both days. It was also great to attend a meetup at a local German themed fun event center with billiards and bowling. And I got to eat my first Xis thanks to Vitor’s guidance. I also got to trip in dramatic fashion in Parque Moinhos de Vento while out for a jog with Vitaliy, and thank goodness he was right there to pick me up as any good Developer Advocate would do!

 

 


On a side note, I also was lucky enough to meet some local SAP colleagues who before now I’ve only seen online. Thank you for your time and I wish we had more chances to meet as we are all SAP family.

 

p.s. When Antonia says “Did you see the colleague in the next room with blue hair?” Well I think you know what happened next, right Ângela Schmidt

__

And stop 4 for me of the #LATAM  #Brazil roadshow is in the books. It’s actually the end of the whole tour for the team in Brazil which is sad but was such a great time for the SAP Community . A special shoutout to Vitória Cristofolli  and Vitor Ugo Fiori  for their support.

Arriving from Porto Alegre to #Recife was such a dramatic change. A northern coastal town with such a different look and feel to the architecture and of course the ocean views

I did a quick tour of the downtown and actually got to see the first synagogue ever built in the Americas (Synagoga Kahal Zur Israel) which showed me so much personal history I never thought I’d experience and will share with my Rabbi.



Then it was back to work. Me, Nora von Thenen  , Vitaliy Rudnytskiy  and Antonia Zorn  were hosted by the amazing team at Accenture led by João Otto Gethmann Jr  .


The attendees were so warm and attentive and enjoyed learning from Witalij on day 1 and Nora on day 2. Me and Antonia shared about the community and also had the pleasure of seeing so many locals at our meetup on a rooftop.

Stories were shared, local food and drink consumed and code discussions all around.  SAP Community, #barbaAzul shall return…

entry.title: SAP Joule for Developers: Your AI Copilot for Smarter, Faster SAP Development
entry.content:
Introduction

Every SAP developer knows the feeling — staring at thousands of lines of legacy ABAP code, manually writing unit tests, or spending hours trying to understand a CDS view someone else wrote three years ago. What if you had an AI copilot sitting right beside you in your development environment, understanding your context, generating code, explaining logic, and helping you migrate faster?

That's exactly what SAP Joule for Developers delivers.

Introduced in early 2025 and rapidly expanding its reach, Joule for Developers is SAP's embedded AI capability suite designed specifically for the needs of SAP developers. Whether you work with ABAP, Java, JavaScript, low-code tools, or process automation — Joule is now your intelligent development partner. And the best part? It's currently free of charge.

What Is SAP Joule for Developers?

SAP Joule for Developers is the collective name for design-time AI capabilities across SAP Build and ABAP that help improve developer productivity and proficiency. It covers AI assistance built directly into:

  • SAP Build Code — Full-stack app development with Java, JavaScript, and CAP
  • SAP Build Process Automation — AI-assisted workflow and automation creation
  • SAP Build Apps — Low-code/no-code app development with AI support
  • ABAP Environment — AI capabilities embedded in ABAP Development Tools for Eclipse (ADT)

Important distinction: Joule for Developers focuses exclusively on design-time AI — meaning AI that helps you during development. It does not include runtime AI capabilities like SAP Document AI, SAP AI Core, or SAP Build Work Zone's AI features.

 

Why Joule for Developers Is a Game Changer

Purpose-Built for SAP

Unlike generic AI coding assistants, Joule for Developers is powered by purpose-built large language models specifically trained on SAP code and processes, including a dedicated ABAP LLM trained on millions of lines of enterprise-grade ABAP code. This means:

  • It understands SAP-specific syntax, frameworks, and best practices
  • It respects ABAP language version contexts (e.g., ABAP Cloud vs. classic ABAP)
  • It delivers accurate, contextual results tailored to SAP development scenarios — not generic programming suggestions

Proven Business Impact

SAP reports that Joule for Developers can reduce development costs by up to 30%. Partners and customers who have adopted it are already seeing measurable results:

"Joule for Developers dramatically boosts productivity by enabling developers to quickly generate enterprise-grade apps, including logic, data models, sample data, and test scripts." — Gerard Van Paassen, CEO, Aarini Consulting

"PwC uses Joule for Developers in SAP Build to rapidly deliver innovative SAP solutions. Its generative AI supercharges our developers' output, helping us accelerate award-winning ESG solutions." — Dr. [Name], PwC

 

Key Capabilities: What Can Joule for Developers Do?

  1. Code Generation & Full-Stack App Creation

Generate complete full-stack applications from natural language prompts — including data models, business logic, UI components, and sample data — all following SAP programming models like RAP (RESTful ABAP Programming Model) and CAP (Cloud Application Programming Model).

  1. Predictive Code Completion

Get real-time, context-aware code suggestions as you type in your development environment. Joule predicts and generates subsequent lines of code based on your ABAPDoc comments, existing code patterns, and the ABAP language version of your current development object.

  1. Code Explanation & Understanding

Instantly understand what a piece of code does — even if you didn't write it. Joule can explain:

  • CDS view entities, classes, interfaces, and function modules
  • Legacy ABAP programs and reports
  • Business purpose and implementation logic of unknown code objects

This is invaluable for onboarding new developers and tackling inherited codebases.

  1. Unit Test & CDS Test Generation

Automatically generate ABAP unit tests and CDS unit tests based on your business logic. Catch issues earlier in the lifecycle and ensure more reliable application behavior in production — without manually writing every test case.

  1. Code Refactoring & Optimization

Use natural language prompts to refactor existing code, improve quality, and align with SAP best practices — all while keeping your ABAP cloud-compliant and clean-core-ready.

  1. Process & Automation Generation

In SAP Build Process Automation, Joule can generate complete automation workflows, business rules, forms, and scripts from natural language queries — dramatically reducing time spent on repetitive configuration tasks.

  1. OData Consumption Assistance

Get AI-assisted guidance on consuming OData services in your ABAP Cloud developments — one of the more complex tasks for developers new to ABAP Cloud patterns.

 

Big News: Joule for Developers Expands to Private Cloud

One of the most significant milestones in 2025 was the expansion of Joule for Developers ABAP AI capabilities to SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition 2025. Previously available only for BTP ABAP Environment and SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud Edition, this expansion means private cloud customers can now also leverage the full power of AI-assisted ABAP development.

What's New for Private Cloud?

This release brings a full set of ABAP Cloud development capabilities to Private Cloud 2025, including:

  • Joule Copilot integrated into ABAP Development Tools for Eclipse
  • Predictive Code Completion
  • ABAP / CDS Code Explanation
  • ABAP Unit Test Generation
  • CDS Unit Test Generation
  • ABAP Cloud Application Generation
  • OData Consumption support

Custom Code Migration: A Major Leap Forward

The Private Cloud release also introduces the first wave of Custom Code Migration capabilities — a set of AI-powered features designed to accelerate the move from classic SAP ERP to SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition:

Documentation Chat: Get instant, accurate answers about SAP S/4HANA simplifications and the impact they have on your existing custom code. No more digging through simplification lists manually.

ABAP Program Explain: Automatically understand the business purpose and implementation logic of legacy ABAP programs and reports — essential when dealing with decades-old custom code that lacks documentation.

Explain for SAP S/4HANA Test Cockpit Issues: Diagnose and resolve ATC (ABAP Test Cockpit) findings related to S/4HANA simplification items, with step-by-step guidance on how to fix each issue using context from simplification cookbooks.

This is a massive time-saver for organizations currently on their S/4HANA transformation journey.

The ABAP AI SDK: Build Your Own AI-Powered ABAP Applications

Beyond the developer tooling, Joule for Developers also includes the ABAP AI SDK — a standardized SDK that allows ABAP developers to integrate AI capabilities directly into their own custom ABAP applications and extensions.

The ABAP AI SDK is:

  • Used internally by SAP for its own ABAP AI functionality
  • Easy to configure and consume — no deep AI expertise required
  • Available across environments — BTP ABAP Environment, S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition 2025, and even on-premise via Transport-based Correction Instructions (TCIs)

This opens the door to building intelligent, AI-powered custom applications on top of SAP AI Core — directly from within ABAP.

Where Is Joule for Developers Available?

Environment

Availability

SAP BTP ABAP Environment

✅ Generally Available

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

✅ Generally Available

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition 2025

✅ Generally Available (as of Oct 2025)

SAP S/4HANA On-Premise (lower releases)

✅ Via Transport-based Correction Instructions (TCI) — SAP Note 3513374

SAP Build Code

✅ Enabled by default

SAP Build Process Automation

✅ Enable from Build Lobby

SAP Build Apps

✅ Enable from Build Lobby

How to Get Access: It's Free!

SAP Joule for Developers is available free of charge through Q3 2026 — a promotional offer that SAP has extended to ensure every customer and partner can explore and adopt AI-assisted development without commercial barriers.

For SAP Build (Build Code, Build Process Automation, Build Apps):

  • SAP Build Code: AI capabilities are enabled by default when you sign up
  • SAP Build Process Automation & SAP Build Apps: Enable from the Build Lobby → Control Tower → Generative AI Settings → Toggle "Enable AI" → Accept SAP AI Terms

For ABAP Environment (BTP / Public Cloud / Private Cloud 2025):

  1. Request the no-cost service SKU via your SAP Account Executive or through the Manage My Services App:
    • Customers: Material Number 8019124
    • TDD Partners: Material Number 8019541
  2. Once the order is processed, ABAP development systems are enabled within 48 hours
  3. Assign required authorizations to your development users

Note: A valid SAP Build or ABAP license is required as a prerequisite. After the promotional period ends, a commercial model based on AI Units will apply.

Frequently Asked Questions

Q: Is Joule for Developers the same as SAP Joule (the end-user copilot)?

No. SAP Joule is the end-user AI copilot embedded across SAP applications for business users. Joule for Developers is specifically for developers — focused on design-time activities like code generation, testing, and code understanding within development tools.

Q: Does it work with non-SAP systems too?

Yes. Joule capabilities in SAP Build can help build and extend applications across both SAP and non-SAP systems, as confirmed by partners like PwC and Deloitte.

Q: What about data privacy and security?

Joule for Developers uses SAP's purpose-built, enterprise-grade AI models within the SAP AI Core infrastructure. Accepting the SAP AI Terms is a prerequisite for activation, ensuring appropriate governance.

Q: Can I use it on lower releases of S/4HANA Private Cloud or On-Premise?

Yes, for lower releases of S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition and On-Premise systems, ABAP AI capabilities can be deployed via Transport-based Correction Instructions (TCIs). Refer to SAP Note 3513374 for details.

Getting Started: Your Action Plan

Ready to bring AI into your ABAP development workflow? Here's how to start today:

  1. Check your environment: Confirm you're on a supported platform (BTP ABAP, S/4HANA Public or Private Cloud 2025, or On-Premise with TCIs)
  2. Request the license: Contact your SAP Account Executive for the no-cost SKU (8019124 for customers)
  3. Enable AI in SAP Build: Follow the quick setup in the Build Lobby if you're using Build Process Automation or Build Apps
  4. Open ADT (Eclipse): Once enabled, the Joule copilot will be available directly in your ABAP Development Tools
  5. Explore: Start with code explanation on a legacy program or generate a simple unit test — the results will speak for themselves

Conclusion

SAP Joule for Developers represents a fundamental shift in how SAP developers work. From generating entire ABAP Cloud applications to explaining decades-old legacy code and accelerating S/4HANA migrations, the capabilities are both broad and deeply SAP-specific.

With the expansion to Private Cloud 2025, the introduction of Custom Code Migration features, and the availability of the ABAP AI SDK — SAP is making clear that AI assistance isn't a future concept for SAP developers; it's available right now, and it's free.

If you're an ABAP developer, a Build developer, or an SAP architect helping your organization stay competitive in the AI era — there has never been a better time to get started with Joule for Developers.

Have you tried Joule for Developers yet? Share your experience in the comments — I'd love to hear which capabilities are making the biggest difference for your team!

References:

  • SAP Community Blog: SAP Joule for Developers Expands to Private Cloud
  • SAP Community FAQ: SAP Joule for Developers — Features, Access and More
  • SAP News: Introducing Joule for Developers: AI-Powered Capabilities Across SAP
  • SAP Product Page: SAP Joule for Developers
  • SAP Note: 3513374 (TCI-based deployment for lower S/4HANA releases)

entry.title: Unlock the Power of Intelligent Document Processing with SAP Document AI and Generative AI
entry.content:
Introduction

In today's fast-paced enterprise world, documents are everywhere — invoices, purchase orders, delivery notes, contracts, and more. Processing them manually is time-consuming, error-prone, and simply not scalable. That's where SAP Document AI comes in.

SAP Document AI (formerly known as Document Information Extraction) is a service on the SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP) that uses machine learning and, more recently, Large Language Models (LLMs) to automatically extract structured information from business documents. With the integration of Generative AI, it has become more powerful than ever — capable of handling not just standard document types, but virtually any custom document you throw at it.

In this blog post, I'll walk you through how SAP Document AI works, how to leverage Generative AI for document extraction, and why this matters for your SAP landscape.

What Is SAP Document AI?

SAP Document AI is an intelligent document processing service that enables businesses to:

  • Automatically extract key fields from documents (header fields, line items, etc.)
  • Support standard document types like invoices, payment advices, and purchase orders out of the box
  • Handle custom document types (delivery notes, birth certificates, contracts, etc.) using schema-based extraction
  • Integrate seamlessly with SAP S/4HANA, SAP Ariba, and other SAP solutions via API

At its core, the service uses pretrained machine learning models that are globally trained on vast datasets of business documents. But the real game-changer? The addition of Generative AI.

Generative AI + SAP Document AI: A Powerful Combination

Why Generative AI?

Traditional ML models are great for standard document types where the structure is well-known. But what about non-standard documents that vary in layout, language, or structure? This is where LLMs shine.

By combining SAP Document AI with Generative AI (LLMs), you can:

  • Define custom schemas for any document type without training a dedicated ML model
  • Use natural language descriptions as prompts to guide the LLM on what to extract
  • Get accurate extraction results even for documents with varying formats
  • Reduce the time-to-value dramatically — no lengthy ML training cycles required

Two Extraction Approaches in One Service

SAP Document AI now supports a hybrid approach:

Setup Type

Method

Best For

auto with Default Extractor

Pretrained ML Model

Standard fields in invoices, POs, payment advices

auto without Default Extractor

Generative AI (LLM)

Custom fields, non-standard documents

manual

Template-based

Highly structured, fixed-layout documents

This flexibility means you can mix and match extraction methods within the same schema, getting the best of both worlds.

How It Works: Step-by-Step

Here's a simplified walkthrough of how to use SAP Document AI with Generative AI:

Step 1: Set Up Your Environment

Start with an SAP BTP Trial or Free Tier account. Use the SAP BTP Booster to automatically provision the SAP Document AI service instance and subscribe to the Basic UI application. This sets up everything you need to get started quickly.

Step 2: Create a Schema

A schema defines the structure of the data you want to extract. Think of it as a template that tells the AI what information to look for.

  • Navigate to Schema Configuration in the SAP Document AI Basic UI

 

  • Click Create and give your schema a name (e.g., delivery_note_schema)

 

  • Select the appropriate Document Type — choose Custom for non-standard documents, or a specific type like Invoice for standard documents

Step 3: Define Your Fields

This is where Generative AI truly shines. For each field you want to extract:

 

  • Enter a field name (e.g., vendor_name, total_amount, delivery_date)
  • Choose a data type (string, number, date, currency, etc.)
  • Add a description — this is used as a prompt for the LLM

💡 Pro Tip: The description field is your prompt to the LLM. Write it as if you're explaining what you want to extract to a person with no prior context. The more specific you are, the better the extraction results!

For example:

  • Field: invoice_number
  • Description: "The unique identifier assigned to this invoice by the issuing company, typically found in the header area of the document."

Step 4: Activate the Schema

Once all fields are added, Activate the schema to move it from DRAFT to ACTIVE status. Only active schemas can be used for document extraction.

 

Step 5: Upload Documents and Extract

  • Navigate to the Document section in the UI

 

  • Upload your document (PDF, image, etc.)
  • Select your schema
  • The service processes the document and returns extracted field values in seconds

 

Real-World Use Cases

The combination of SAP Document AI and Generative AI opens up exciting possibilities:

  1. Accounts Payable Automation Automatically extract invoice details (vendor, amount, tax, line items) and post directly to SAP S/4HANA — reducing manual data entry and processing time.
  2. Logistics & Supply Chain Process delivery notes and shipping documents of any format, extracting quantities, dates, and goods information without custom ML training.
  3. HR & Compliance Extract information from onboarding documents, certificates, or contracts — even in multiple languages and varying formats.
  4. Cross-Industry Document Processing From healthcare (medical reports) to legal (contracts) to finance (bank statements) — if it's a document, SAP Document AI can handle it.

Integration with SAP Ecosystem

SAP Document AI isn't a standalone tool — it's designed to integrate deeply into the SAP ecosystem:

  • REST API: Integrate with any application using the standard SAP Document AI REST API via Swagger UI
  • SAP BTP CAP Applications: Build custom CAP-based applications that consume the Document AI service for automated document workflows
  • SAP Build: Use SAP Build Code or SAP Build Apps to create no-code/low-code automations powered by Document AI
  • SAP S/4HANA & Ariba: Direct integration for invoice processing and procurement document handling

Getting Started: Try It for Free

The best part? You can try SAP Document AI today at no cost using the SAP BTP Trial or Free Tier:

  1. Sign up for SAP BTP Trial
  2. Use the SAP BTP Booster to set up SAP Document AI
  3. Follow the hands-on tutorial on SAP Developers: Extract Information from Custom Documents with Generative AI

The trial provides a sandbox environment where you can experiment with both standard and custom document types using real sample files.

Key Takeaways

  • SAP Document AI combines machine learning and Generative AI (LLMs) for flexible, accurate document extraction
  • Custom schemas allow you to extract information from virtually any document type — no ML training required
  • Field descriptions act as LLM prompts — writing good descriptions directly improves extraction accuracy
  • The service is available on SAP BTP Trial and Free Tier, making it easy to experiment and build proof-of-concepts
  • Deep integration with the SAP ecosystem enables seamless end-to-end document automation

Conclusion

SAP Document AI with Generative AI is a transformative capability for any organization dealing with high volumes of documents. By removing the need for custom ML training while still delivering accurate structured extraction, it dramatically lowers the barrier to intelligent document processing.

Whether you're looking to automate accounts payable, streamline logistics, or build a custom document processing workflow — SAP Document AI gives you the tools to do it efficiently on SAP BTP.

I encourage you to try the hands-on tutorial and explore what's possible. Have questions or want to share your experience? Drop a comment below — I'd love to hear how you're using SAP Document AI in your projects!

References:

  • SAP Developers Tutorial: Extract Information from Custom Documents with Generative AI and SAP Document AI
  • SAP Developers Tutorial: Extract Information from Standard Documents with Generative AI and SAP Document AI
  • SAP Document AI on SAP BTP: Free Tier Setup

entry.title: SoCal Community Event : SAP Business Data Cloud " From Pipelines to Data Products "
entry.content:
We recently hosted a successful SoCal SAP Community event at HanaHaus, proudly sponsored by SAP and Artisan Edge. The event brought together architects, data leaders, and practitioners for an engaging and collaborative session

SAP SoCal Group

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

The focus of the session was SAP Business Data Cloud  (BDC) and the evolving shift toward data products as a foundation for modern data architectures. Rather than a one-way presentation, the discussion quickly became highly interactive, with participants sharing real-world challenges, perspectives, and use cases.

Together, we:

  • Explored the architectural evolution of BDC
  • Discussed the transition from traditional pipelines to data product thinking
  • Examined how zero-copy integration is reshaping data access and AI enablement

What stood out most was the level of engagement. Attendees actively contributed insights on data ownership, governance, and the practical realities of adopting a data mesh–aligned operating model.

 

Continue the Conversation

We’d love to keep the momentum going:

  • What challenges are you facing in moving toward a data product or data mesh architecture?
  • Where do you see the biggest friction in your current data landscape?

Feel free to share your thoughts in the comments or reach out to connect. Your feedback will help shape upcoming SoCal SAP Community sessions—and we hope to see you at the next event!

Speakers : @VijayetaSharma ,@EganBosch 

Coordinators : @VijayetaSharma ,@AndrewCruikshank 

Sponsors : @SAP , Artisan Edge

entry.title: Wiosenne Spotkanie Katowickiej Grupy SAP Community - Kwiecień 2026
entry.content:
Relacja ze spotkania SAP Stammtisch – Katowice, 15.04.2026

Kwietniowe spotkanie katowickiego SAP Stammtisch po raz kolejny pokazało, że siła tej inicjatywy tkwi w ludziach i otwartej wymianie doświadczeń. 15 kwietnia spotkaliśmy się w sprawdzonym gronie, ale co szczególnie cieszy – dołączyły do nas również trzy nowe osoby, które od razu wniosły świeżą perspektywę i energię do dyskusji.

Już od pierwszych minut było widać, że nie będzie to „zwykłe” spotkanie. Rozmowy szybko zeszły na konkretne, techniczne tematy – od podejścia do clean code w projektach SAP, przez praktyczne doświadczenia z SAP BTP, aż po realne wyzwania, które pojawiają się podczas wdrożeń. To właśnie ten miks - teorii, praktyki i szczerych opinii z projektów - sprawia, że Stammtisch ma swoją unikalną wartość.

Nie zabrakło również dyskusji o tym, co zmienia się w świecie SAP na bieżąco. Uczestnicy dzielili się obserwacjami dotyczącymi rozwoju produktów, zmian w podejściu do architektury oraz kierunków, w jakich zmierza ekosystem SAP. Takie rozmowy często wykraczają poza „oficjalną dokumentację” i pozwalają spojrzeć na technologie z zupełnie innej, bardziej praktycznej strony.

Szczególnie wartościowy był fakt, że nowe osoby nie tylko słuchały, ale aktywnie uczestniczyły w rozmowach zadając pytania, dzieląc się własnymi doświadczeniami i wnosząc nowe wątki. To najlepszy dowód na to, że SAP Stammtisch jest miejscem otwartym niezależnie od poziomu doświadczenia czy roli w projektach, gdzie wszyscy uczestnicy chętnie ze sobą rozmawiają.

Co dalej?

Nie zwalniamy tempa! Kolejne spotkanie zapowiada się równie ciekawie i odbędzie się już 24.06.2026.

Planujemy kontynuować tematykę AI w SAP, ze szczególnym naciskiem na SAP Joule, który budzi coraz większe zainteresowanie i rodzi sporo pytań w kontekście praktycznego wykorzystania.

Dodatkowo na tapetę trafi bardzo aktualny temat:

  • zmiany w API Policy (v4 2026) i ich wpływ na istniejące integracje klientów

To zagadnienie już teraz wywołuje sporo dyskusji w społeczności i z pewnością będzie okazją do wymiany konkretnych doświadczeń oraz potencjalnych strategii dostosowania się do nowych zasad.

Dlaczego warto przyjść?

Bo to nie jest konferencja ani webinar trzymający się sztywnych ram i wąskiej tematyki.
To miejsce, gdzie:

  • możesz swobodnie pogadać o realnych problemach projektowych
  • poznasz ludzi z różnych obszarów SAP
  • dowiesz się, jak inni rozwiązują podobne wyzwania
  • i co najważniejsze, poszerzysz nowe kontakty

Każde kolejne spotkanie pokazuje, że społeczność rośnie, a wraz z nią rośnie poziom i różnorodność dyskusji.

Jeśli jeszcze nie byłeś – to dobry moment, żeby dołączyć.
Jeśli byłeś – wiesz, że warto wrócić! 🙂

entry.title: SAP Customer Conversations LIVE. Реальний досвід та результати
entry.content:
SAP Customer Conversations LIVE — формат, у якому референс-клієнт SAP представляє кейс використання рішення, ключові моменти впровадження та досягнуті бізнес-результати — після чого відбувається жива дискусія та інтерактивна Q&A сесія. Саме такий клієнтський досвід прискорює угоди та будує довіру.

Ваші клієнти матимуть змогу познайомитися з реальними кейсами трансформації, поспілкуватися наживо з колегами по галузі та отримати відповіді на запитання щодо ризиків і строків окупності. Для вас це означає довіру клієнта з першого кроку, переконливі аргументи проти будь-яких заперечень, коротші цикли продажів і більше виграних угод.

⌚Час подій вказано за київським часом

🌍Мова заходів — англійська

Найближчі події — за напрямками рішень:

Supply Chain Management

  • 28 травня, 17:00–18:00 
    SAP IBP @ Orkla IT AS 
    🏢 Agribusiness | 📊 Large Enterprise | 🎤 Christian Hjerp — Solution Architect

    Orkla використовує SAP IBP з 2016–2017 років — система охоплює     5 планових областей і підтримує 13 компаній групи.
    На цій сесії ви дізнаєтесь, чому Orkla обрала SAP IBP, як вона забезпечує міжкорпоративну координацію та щоденне планування, як IBP S&OP допомагає будувати реалістичні плани для виробництва, а також почуєте про інтеграцію та шлях впровадження, винесені уроки, та плани на майбутнє і переваги для бізнесу й кінцевих користувачів. Q&A сесія на завершення.

SAP SuccessFactors HCM

  • 27 травня, 18:00–19:00 
    SAP SuccessFactors Performance Management @ IBM 
    🏢 High Tech  | 📊 Enterprise | 🎤 Simone Carmona Steffen - Portfolio Leader, SAP SuccessFactors - HR Technology 

    IBM     переосмислила HR як ключовий драйвер продуктивності у глобальному масштабі. За допомогою SAP SuccessFactors компанія об'єднала сотні систем, впровадила AI у щоденні процеси та уніфікувала досвід співробітників у 83 країнах.
    На цій сесії ви дізнаєтесь, як IBM перебудувала процес Performance Management для     275 000+ співробітників у понад 80 країнах в єдиній системі, як компанія однією з перших впровадила новий формат управління ефективністю, як IBM бачить розвиток Performance у контексті ширшої Talent-моделі, а також які AI-інновації вже використовуються та плануються на майбутнє. В кінці — жива Q&A сесія.

Business Technology Platform

  • 27 травня, 17:00–18:00 
    SAP BTP @ Caverion Oyj 
    🏢 Engineering/Construction/Operations | 📊 Large Enterprise | 🎤 Alex Vasyliev - SAP Platform Manager

    Caverion Oyj проектує, будує та обслуговує інженерні системи будівель по всьому світу. На цій сесії Alex Vasyliev поділиться досвідом використання SAP BTP у рамках проєкту Assembling Caverion Group (ACG) — від впровадження та архітектури до оновлень та винесених уроків. Це чесний, практичний погляд на те, що спрацювало добре, що потребувало коригування, і як SAP BTP допомагає ACG відстежувати життєвий цикл програмного забезпечення SAP. Завершується живою Q&A сесією.

Поділіться цими ексклюзивними сесіями з клієнтами вже сьогодні — і дайте їм можливість почути реальні історії успіху від тих, хто вже впровадив SAP. 🌐

entry.title: SAP Community Enhancements: Improved Discovery and a Cleaner Experience
entry.content:
We are happy to introduce our latest Community enhancements, designed to streamline user engagement and modernize content discovery. By reducing friction for anonymous visitors with more accessible "Join" and "Subscribe" prompts, the platform now offers a smoother path to participation, complemented by a refined "Recent Activity" feed that prioritizes timely, high-engagement discussions from the last 60 days. Beyond these navigation improvements, users will find a more organized Events Calendar, improved read/unread visibility for blog posts, and a decluttered homepage interface that emphasizes essential announcements. Together, these UI refinements and consistency fixes ensure a more intuitive, stable, and focused environment for both new and long-standing community members.

Check out all the details now:
 
Easier Joining and Following (Even When Not Logged In)
New and anonymous visitors are now better guided to engage with the community:
  • Join Group buttons are now visible even when you are not logged in
    If you click “Join” while logged out, you’ll be prompted to log in or register, and the join action will complete automatically after login.
  • Subscribe buttons are now visible when logged out
    On tag pages, users can immediately see the option to follow a topic, encouraging discovery and return visits.
This makes it clearer how to get involved and reduces friction for new users.
 
Improved Content Discovery in Recent Activity Feeds
Recent Activity feeds across the main interaction styles have been refined to highlight more relevant and timely content:
  • Most viewed, most commented, and most liked filters now focus on recently created content (last 60 days), instead of pulling very old posts.
  • This ensures that highly engaged recent discussions are easier to find.
You’ll now see fresher, more meaningful content without changing filters manually.
 
A Cleaner, More Relevant Events Calendar
We’ve improved how events appear in the main Community Events Calendar:
  • Events created within Location Groups are now more selectively promoted to the main "All Location Events" community calendar
  • The main calendar focuses on broader, community‑relevant location events
  • All Group events remain fully visible inside their respective Groups
  • Existing event boards remain fully visible
Easier discovery of location events that are relevant to the wider community.
 
Better Read / Unread Visibility
To help you keep track of what’s new:
  • Read / unread indicators are now available for Blog Posts in tile view, extending the functionality already available in other feeds.
It’s now easier to spot new content you haven’t read yet.
 
Clearer Dates and More Consistent Formatting
Several refinements improve clarity and consistency:
  • “Last edited” dates now respect your personal date format preferences
    This applies especially to Knowledge Base (TKB) content.
  • Accepted solution indicators are now consistently aligned, even when titles span multiple lines.
Dates and visual cues should now feel more predictable and easier to scan.
 
Cleaner Homepage and Navigation Adjustments
To reduce clutter and improve focus:
  • The “users online” statistic has been removed from the homepage, simplifying the layout.
  • Content building blocks (CCB widgets) were repositioned to better highlight important announcements now that carousels have been removed.
Key information is easier to spot without competing distractions.
 
Polished UI Fixes and Small Improvements
A set of targeted refinements improves visual quality and usability:
  • Improved alignment and spacing in notification settings
  • Minor icon and layout adjustments for better consistency
  • Fixes to ensure interface elements behave the same way across pages
These changes don’t introduce new features, but make the experience feel more stable and refined.
